sections.paragraph:
εὐκόλως adv. (opp. δυσκόλως; since X., Mem. 4, 8, 2; Herm. Wr. 13, 16; PCairMasp 2 III, 23; 4, 19) pert. to being easy, with implication of causing no imposition, easily Hm 12, 3, 5 v.l. (for εὐκόπως).—DELG s.v. κόλον.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

opp. opp. = opposed to, opposite

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

Herm. Wr. Herm. Wr. = Hermetic Writings—List 5

PCairMasp PCairMasp = JMaspero, Papyrus grecs d’époque byzantine—List 4

pert. pert. = pertaining (to)

Hm Hm = Mandates

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 407
